channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Congressional report tallies 42 US aircraft lost or damaged in Operation Epic Fury | Defense News
item.description: Drones took the heaviest hit, accounting for 25 of the 42 aircraft losses listed in the report.

item.title: 📍 Xi’s Flurry of Post-Trump Diplomacy | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Chinese president may be preparing for a rare and momentous trip to Pyongyang.

item.title: ⚛️ IAI’s new Diamond naval offering envisions flexible drones, missiles for small vessels | Breaking Defense
item.description: The plan involves different defensive systems dispersed across small satellite ships, which can be connected to a mother ship in a disaggregated model.

item.title: 🌏 What did the Trump-Xi summit actually achieve? | Atlantic Council
item.description: After months of anticipation, US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping finally met in Beijing. But beyond a few concrete announcements—including a Chinese pledge to buy two hundred Boeing jets—the summit raised more questions than it answered. Together with…

item.title: 🕵️ Helos need upgrades to perform special ops ‘quieter, with fewer aircraft’: PEO Rotary Wing | Breaking Defense
item.description: Special Operations Command’s fleet of MH-60Ms and MH-47Gs — operated by the 160th Nightstalkers Special Operations Aviation Regiment (SOAR) — could benefit from technology injections coming from the MV-75 Cheyenne, said PEO Rotary Wing Steve Smith.

item.title: 🌍 Libya and Syria join Turkey’s flagship military exercise in historic firsts | Defense News
item.description: Ankara framed both nations’ attendance as part of its broader military-restructuring and advisory programs in each country.

item.title: 🏛️ Lockheed breaks ground on new THAAD interceptor plant, as Pentagon pushes for more weapons production | Breaking Defense
item.description: “These aren’t just ideas or papers going back and forth,” Lockheed CEO Jim Taiclet said of the Pentagon’s munitions ramp. “We know it’s going to be good, and we know it’s going to happen.”

item.title: 📍 Inside Sen. Ted Budd’s plan to boost airpower, pilot retention | Breaking Defense
item.description: North Carolina Sen. Ted Budd explains why he recently introduced three bills intended to boost US fighter inventory and improve pilot retention.

item.title: 🏛️ General Atomics CCA drone returns to flight | Breaking Defense
item.description: Air Force Col. Timothy Helfrich said the response to the drone’s April 6 crash “validates our approach to accept acquisition/test risk instead of operational risk allowing us to accelerate the program towards fielding.”

item.title: ⚛️ F-35B conducts flight test with British SPEAR mini-cruise missile | Breaking Defense
item.description: The first flight milestone comes four years later than originally planned, after an initial delay caused by technical troubles.

item.title: 📍 Ethiopia and Eritrea are on the brink of war again | Atlantic Council
item.description: There is a real risk that the current war of words spirals out of control. The post Ethiopia and Eritrea are on the brink of war again appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 China’s Fallen Generals Are Getting Unexpectedly Harsh Punishments | Foreign Policy
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping is raising the floor for punishing purge targets.

item.title: 🌏 Taiwan says it has not been told by US of arms sales delays | Defense News
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said following a summit with China’s Xi Jinping in Beijing last week that he was undecided on whether to approve it.

item.title: 🏴 Russian nukes threaten ‘almost all’ US cities, Norway’s FM says, highlighting NATO’s value | Breaking Defense
item.description: “It is good that Europe is doing more, but there’s a need to remind our good friends on the other side of the Atlantic that NATO is also good for them,” said the Nordic official.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s Parliament Is Pulling Back on LGBTQ Rights as Courts and Citizens Move Forward | Just Security
item.description: Ukraine’s Supreme Court recognized a same-sex family, but parliament is moving to block legal recognition for LGBTQ+ couples. The post Ukraine’s Parliament Is Pulling Back on LGBTQ Rights as Courts and Citizens Move Forward appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🔐 Is the United States on the Verge of Military Intervention in Cuba? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Seizing Castro could prove more costly and less effective than the capture of Maduro.

item.title: 📍 To Memorialize the Fallen, Renew the Pursuit of Peace | Just Security
item.description: This Memorial Day, to honor the memory of those who gave their lives in war, Americans should consider how to help mold a more peaceful future at home and abroad. The post To Memorialize the Fallen, Renew the Pursuit of Peace appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌏 Project Anthracite: Analyzing North Korean Patents and Academic Journals for Evidence of Chemical Weapons Potential | 38 North
item.description: This research draws on Project Anthracite, a multiyear initiative funded by Global Affairs ... The post Project Anthracite: Analyzing North Korean Patents and Academic Journals for Evidence of Chemical Weapons Potential appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🕵️ SOCOM begins fielding new battlefield biometrics system | Defense News
item.description: Reveal Technology’s Identifi platform allows operators to collect fingerprints, facial scans, iris data and voice recognition in the field.

item.title: 🏴 No Limits, No Good Options | Foreign Policy
item.description: Putin’s visit to China shows that Russia is getting sucked deeper into a profoundly unequal relationship.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian Drone Incursions into Baltic States, Russian Electronic Warfare Countermeasures, and International Law | Just Security
item.description: Experts unpack the international law implications of recent incursions of Ukrainian drones into the airspace of Baltic countries due to Russian electronic warfare tactics. The post Ukrainian Drone Incursions into Baltic States, Russian Electronic Warfare Countermeasures, and…

item.title: 📍 Early Edition: May 22, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: U.S. CARIBBEAN AND PACIFIC OPERATIONS President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio yesterday again raised the…

item.title: 📍 Violence Looms Over Colombia’s Election | Foreign Policy
item.description: Three presidential candidates lead the field ahead of a first-round vote.

item.title: 🏛️ RIAT canceled: Middle East ‘situation’ forces collapse of major UK air show | Breaking Defense
item.description: “This has not been an easy decision,” but it was made after “extensive discussions” with the RAF and the US Air Force, said organizers of the Royal International Air Tattoo today.

item.title: 🏴 Vyshyvanka Day celebrates Ukrainian identity amid Russia’s genocidal war | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine's annual Vyshyvanka Day holiday has emerged as a simple yet profound grassroots celebration of Ukrainian national identity at a time when Russia is attempting to destroy Ukraine as a state and as a nation, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Vyshyvanka Day celebrates…

item.title: 🛡️ The future of US counterterrorism: An expert assessment of the 2026 White House strategy | Atlantic Council
item.description: Members of the Atlantic Council Counterterrorism Project dig into the Trump administration’s new US Counterterrorism Strategy. The post The future of US counterterrorism: An expert assessment of the 2026 White House strategy appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Geopolitical Chaos is Wrecking Pandemic Agreements | Foreign Policy
item.description: U.S. dysfunction is undercutting attempts at equality.

item.title: 🏴 Sovereignty can’t be vibecoded: Why Europe must physically build to ensure resilience | Breaking Defense
item.description: Amid debates over budget and policy, Europe must prioritize building the physical infrastructure required to sustain technological and defense capabilities at scale, argue Elin Hammarberg and Carina Zaring.

item.title: 🏴 Inside Ukraine’s Battlefield Innovation Loop | War on the Rocks
item.description: This exclusive Cogs of War interview is with Catarina Buchatskiy, the co-founder and director of analytics at the Snake Island Institute, a Kyiv-based defense analytics center, and Viktoriia Honcharuk, the institute’s director of defense technologies. We asked them to share…

item.title: 🔐 The Pentagon Still Cannot Manage Cyber Talent at Scale. Here’s the Fix. | War on the Rocks
item.description: The Department of Defense does not primarily have a cyber recruiting problem — it has a cyber talent management problem. The military already possesses serious qualification frameworks, scholarship programs, credentialing systems, and selection tools. What it still lacks is a…
